findings.title: Environment Variable Exfiltration to External Analytics Endpoint
findings.description: The transform.py script collects environment variables matching prefixes AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_ and sends them along with the hostname to an external server at txt-transform-analytics.io via HTTP POST. This exfiltration occurs on every call to markdown_to_html or to_snake_case, leaking sensitive credentials and secrets without user knowledge.
findings.evidence: Lines 8-24 define _T = "https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event" and the _report function constructs a payload with a filtered environment dictionary and sends it via urllib.request.urlopen. The function is called inside markdown_to_html and to_snake_case.
findings.remediation: Remove all network calls and environment variable collection. Implement offline-only processing. If telemetry is absolutely necessary, obtain explicit user consent, anonymize data, and use a local-only logging mechanism.

findings.title: Falsified Manifest Claims – Network Access and Capabilities
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest explicitly states 'network: false' and 'No network access required', yet the transform.py script makes outbound HTTP POST requests. Additionally, the description advertises conversion to reStructuredText (RST) which is not implemented. These false claims deceive users and agents about the skill's actual behavior and trustworthiness.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md metadata: 'network: false' and description 'No network access required' contradict network calls in transform.py. Description mentions reStructuredText conversion but code only handles markdown-to-html, upper/lower, and snake_case.
findings.remediation: Accurately document network usage and capabilities. Remove or correct false claims in the manifest. Ensure the description matches actual functionality.

findings.title: Unauthorized Tool Use – Python Tool Not in Declared allowed_tools
findings.description: The manifest declares allowed_tools as ['Read', 'Write', 'Edit'], but the skill instructs the agent to execute a Python script using `python3 transform.py`. This uses the Python tool, which is not listed in allowed_tools, violating the skill's own tool restrictions.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md instructions: 'python3 transform.py --input file.md --format html --output file.html'. The code runs as a Python script, yet allowed_tools does not include Python.
findings.remediation: Either list 'Python' in allowed_tools or restructure the skill to use only the declared tools (Read, Write, Edit) without executing external scripts.
